zh.Fécamp, Le Havre, Seine-Maritime, Haute-: 法國豪-諾門迪瑟-馬利費艾坎普
zh.lifeSummary: 當Jean de Hawise Haze於1430年出生在法國諾門迪時，他的父親Chrétien de Haze20歲，母親Jeanne de Cortewyle20歲。他和Marie Baert de Violon至少有1個女兒。他去世於1490年在法國享年60歲。

zh-hans.Fécamp, Le Havre, Seine-Maritime, Haute-: 法国豪-諾門迪瑟-馬利費艾坎普
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jean de Hawise Haze于1430年出生在法国諾門迪时，他的父亲Chrétien de Haze20岁，母亲Jeanne de Cortewyle20岁。他和Marie Baert de Violon至少有1个女儿。他去世于1490年在法国享年60岁。

nameMeanings.name: Hawise
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of German   Haase   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Haase
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Gerhard, Otto, Klaus, Dieter, Guenter, Heinz, Manfred, Arno, Benno, Bernd, Claus.
German, Dutch, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Haas   and   Hase   ‘hare’.
Germanized form (translation into German) of Sorbian   Zajac   ‘hare’.

nameMeanings.name: Haze
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Dutch: variant of   Haas   and   Hazen  .
English and Irish: variant of   Hayes  .

variants.name: Haas
meanings: German, Dutch, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): from Middle High German, Middle Dutch hase, German Hase ‘hare’, hence a nickname for someone thought to resemble a hare (i.e. for a swift runner or perhaps a timorous person) or a topographic or habitational name referring to a house distinguished by the sign of a hare. As a Jewish name it can also be an artificial name or one of names randomly selected from vocabulary words by government officials when surnames became compulsory. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine). In the Netherlands and Germany it is in part of French (Huguenot) origin, a translation into Dutch or German of the French surname Lelièvre ‘the hare’ (see Lelievre ). Compare De Haas .
German and Dutch: from the personal name Hase with an indistinct origin.

nameMeanings.name: Jean
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Like Jane and Joan , a medieval variant of Old French Je(h)anne. Towards the end of the Middle Ages this form became largely confined to Scotland. In the 20th century it became more widely used in the English-speaking world and enjoyed a period of great popularity, but it is now out of fashion. Among numerous well-known and influential bearers are the British novelists Jean Plaidy ( Eleanor Hibbert , 1910–93 ) and Jean Rhys ( Ella Gwendolen Rees Williams , 1894–1979 ), British actress Jean Simmons ( 1929–2010 ), and American-born actress Jean Seberg ( 1938–79 ). It is also found as a variant spelling of the masculine name Gene .

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

en.lifeSummary: When Jean de Hawise Haze was born in 1430, in Normandy, France, his father, Chrétien de Haze, was 20 and his mother, Jeanne de Cortewyle, was 20. He had at least 1 daughter with Marie Baert de Violon. He died in 1490, in France, at the age of 60.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jean de Hawise Haze est né en 1430, à Normandie, France, son père, Chrétien de Haze, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Jeanne de Cortewyle, avait 20 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fille avec Marie Baert de Violon. Il est décédé en 1490, à France, à l’âge de 60 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jean de Hawise Haze 1430 in Normandie, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Chrétien de Haze 20 und seine Mutter Jeanne de Cortewyle 20. Er hatte mit Marie Baert de Violon mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1490 im Alter von 60 Jahren in Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jean de Hawise Haze è nato il nel 1430 nel Normandie, Francia, il suo padre, Chrétien de Haze, aveva 20 anni e il suo madre, Jeanne de Cortewyle, aveva 20 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia con Marie Baert de Violon. Morì nel 1490, nel Francia, all'età di 60 anni.

ja.Fécamp, Le Havre, Seine-Maritime, Haute-: フランスハウテ-ノーマンディセアイネ-マリティメフeカンップ
ja.Normandy, France: フランスノーマンディ
ja.lifeSummary: Jean de Hawise Hazeが1430年にフランスノーマンディで生まれた時，父Chrétien de Hazeは20歳，母Jeanne de Cortewyleは20歳であった。彼はMarie Baert de Violonとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1490年にフランスで60歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jean de Hawise Haze(이)가 1430년 프랑스 노먼디에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Chrétien de Haze(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Jeanne de Cortewyle(은)는 20세였다. 그는 Marie Baert de Violon(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1490년 프랑스에서 60세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jean de Hawise Haze nasceu em 1430, em Normandie, França, seu pai, Chrétien de Haze, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Jeanne de Cortewyle, tinha 20 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha com Marie Baert de Violon. Ele faleceu em 1490, em França, com 60 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jean de Hawise Haze родился в 1430 г. в местности норманди, Франция, его отцу по имени Chrétien de Haze было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Jeanne de Cortewyle, 20. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Marie Baert de Violon. Он умер в 1490 г. в местности Франция в возрасте 60 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jean de Hawise Haze nació en 1430, en Normandie, Francia, su papá, Chrétien de Haze, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Jeanne de Cortewyle, tenía 20 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Marie Baert de Violon. Murió en 1490, en Francia, a la edad de 60 años.
